'''Toby Sexton''' (born 1 March 2001) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Sexton was born in [[Sunnybank, Queensland|Sunnybank]], a suburb of [[Brisbane]], Queensland.{{citation needed|date=April 2022}}

==Playing career==

=== 2021 ===
In round 19 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Sexton made his debut for the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]] against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], scoring a try and kicking six goals during a 32–10 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/nrl-premiership/2021/round-19/dragons-v-titans/|title=Dragons v Titans|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-07-25/nrl-live-dragons-titans-bulldogs-sharks-score-updates/100308698/|title=Cronulla Sharks beat Canterbury Bulldogs 44-24, Gold Coast Titans down St George Illawarra Dragons 32-10|newspaper=ABC News|date=25 July 2021 }}</ref>

=== 2022 ===
Sexton played a total of 19 games for the Gold Coast in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 13th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-gold-coast-titans-season-review-justin-holbrook-tino-faasuamaleaui-david-fifita-toby-sexton-jayden-campbell-kieran-foran-jamal-fogarty/news-story/271373bd289c9c920124d2847fde6d5e/|title=Titans' million-dollar man goes missing as Holbrook rues costly mistake: Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=8 September 2022 }}</ref>

=== 2023 ===
On April 9, he scored the match winning try in his NRL recall against the St. George Illawarra Dragons in round 6 of the 2023 NRL season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-04-09 |title=Late Sexton try gets Titans home in afternoon thriller |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/04/09/late-sexton-try-gets-titans-home-in-afternoon-thriller/ |access-date=2023-07-04 |website=National Rugby League |language=en}}</ref> On 4 July, he was released from his contract with the Gold Coast and transferred to the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs immediately, signing a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-07-04 |title=Sexton signs with Canterbury |url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2023/07/04/sexton-signs-with-canterbury/ |access-date=2023-07-04 |website=Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/01/01/2023-nrl-signings-tracker-latest-signings-news-details-contracts-signings-updates/|title=2023 NRL Signings Tracker: Bulldogs snare Sexton; Schuster staying put|website=www.nrl.com|date=13 July 2023 }}</ref>
Sexton made his Canterbury debut in the clubs 36-32 victory over South Sydney in round 19 of the competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/live-nrl-bulldogs-unveil-sixth-starting-halves-combo-of-season-against-undermanned-rabbitohs/news-story/9200c30ee3f9d86aca17687d198801d0/|title=Youngsters shining at Accor as Dogs rookie winger's third try puts game to bed - LIVE|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=8 July 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
Sexton played 16 games for Canterbury in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club qualified for the finals finishing 6th on the table. During the season, Sexton usurped [[Drew Hutchison (rugby league)|Drew Hutchison]] to become the club's first choice halfback. Sexton played in Canterbury's elimination finals loss against Manly.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review-phil-gould-cameron-ciraldo-20240916-p5kb0n.html/|title=Bulldogs left 'frustrated' by semi final defeat as new standards laid bare|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
On 4 July, Sexton signed a two-year deal to join French side the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]. It had been reported a month earlier that Sexton was told his services were no longer required at the end of the [[2025 NRL season]] after the signing of [[Lachlan Galvin]]. Canterbury were sitting at the top of the table at the time of this decision.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-toby-sexton-signs-with-catalans-dragons-contract-bulldogs-latest-news-lachlan-galvin/news-story/6bbb587cb96f3d86c8f833b03ea5a9af/|title=‘He’s a great fit’: Unwanted Dogs halfback Toby Sexton makes call on future with overseas move|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-07-03 |title=Bulldogs playmaker Sexton announces shock Dragons switch {{!}} Sporting News Australia |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/bulldogs-nrl-toby-sexton-catalan-dragons-super-league/b136d170fa42bab3b4feaa4e |access-date=2025-07-04 |website=www.sportingnews.com |language=en-au}}</ref>
Sexton played a total of 18 games for Canterbury in 2025 as the club finished 3rd on the table. Canterbury would be eliminated from the finals in straight sets losing to both Melbourne and Penrith.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2025-the-mole-exclusive-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review-20250922-p5mx5l.html/|title=Clue to what went horribly wrong for Bulldogs emerges in 'unhappy ending'|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref> On 29 November 2025, it was reported that Sexton had signed a two-year deal starting in 2027 with the [[Perth Bears]] becoming a marquee signing for the incoming club. Sexton signed this contract without having played a single match for his new club Catalans.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Toby Sexton signs two year deal with Perth Bears|url=https://www.codesports.com.au/subscribe/news/1/?sourceCode=CSWEB_WRE170_a_TWT&dest=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.codesports.com.au%2Fnrl%2Ftoby-sexton-signs-twoyear-deal-with-perth-bears-as-nrls-newest-team-lands-face-of-the-franchise%2Fnews-story%2Fa3cb2782a3c53e0ec4529f65499cbda4&memtype=anonymous&mode=premium&v21=GROUPA-Segment-2-NOSCORE|website=CODE Sports|access-date=2025-11-29|language=en}}</ref>
